arts.ttl: Newcastle preparing mammoth £80m bid for target that would rather move to Liverpool
arts.sum: Newcastle haven’t given up hope of landing Crystal Palace centre-back, Marc Guehi, though it’s believed the defender would prefer a move to Liverpool. The player has already admitted that the speculation surrounding his future last summer was tough to deal with. Given their own defensive problems, Man United were also rumoured to be sniffing around […] The post Newcastle preparing mammoth £80m bid for target that would rather move to Liverpool appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Man United transfer news: Red Devils ahead of Newcastle United in race for former Manchester City star
arts.sum: Manchester United are reportedly ready to leapfrog Newcastle United and sign Bayern Munich forward LEroy Sane should an opportunity present itself.

arts.ttl: Alexander Isak contract warning to Newcastle as Arsenal and Chelsea eye swoop
arts.sum: Isak is said to be currently earning £120,000 per week, and there have been suggestions that Newcastle could hand Gordon and the Sweden international a pay increase that seems them earn a weekly wage of £200,000 - more than Bruno Guimareas, the best-paid player currently. A large number of the comments centred around whether or not Isak and Gordon deserve to be earning £200,000 per week at Newcastle.

arts.ttl: Fresh details emerge from Anthony Gordon Liverpool deal that would have rocked Newcastle
arts.sum: With one star that was sold to prevent points being docked returning this weekend in Yankuba Minteh, a player Howe wanted to keep, the Newcastle head coach told Chronicle Live: "The only two deals we had on the table were for Elliot and Yankuba at that time. When asked about the sale of returning Minteh, Howe said' "In my opinion, it was the right thing to do but it hurt to do it.

arts.ttl: The 'impress me' message issued to Newcastle United duo Sean Longstaff and Joe Willock
arts.sum: Newcastle United boss Eddie Howe knows that there is a danger of "frustration" setting in around the Toon camp in the next few weeks. The Newcastle chief said: "I think we saw a positive impact from both midfield substitutions at Everton, both Sean Longstaff and Joe Willock came on and did well.

arts.ttl: Newcastle vs Brighton: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Seventh hosts sixth in the Premier League on Saturday afternoon when Newcastle United take on Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park. Brighton predicted lineup vs Newcastle (4-2-3-1) : Verbruggen; Veltman, Van Hecke, Dunk, Estupinan; Baleba, Hinshelwood; Minteh, Rutter, Mitoma; Welbeck.

arts.ttl: Tuchel backlash increases Eddie Howe's chances of England job despite FA snub
arts.sum: Eddie Howe may not have even been interviewed for the England job this time around but don't bet against the Newcastle United boss being the man to replace Thomas Tuchel should it all go wrong for the German coach in the next 18 months. The former Chelsea boss' appointment this week ends the already withering speculation over Howe possibly swapping St James' Park for Wembley following Gareth Southgate's summer exit.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United squad revealed for Brighton test as Eddie Howe handed injury boost
arts.sum: Miguel Almiron, Harvey Barnes, Jacob Murphy and Anthony Gordon are all competing for the wide roles and, even discounting Miley, Howe has five strong midfield options in Bruno Guimaraes, Sandro Tonali, Joelinton, Sean Longstaff and Joe Willock. It might be a'simple' case of one in, one out for Eddie Howe and Newcastle United as he makes the final preparations for today's Premier League game with Brighton and Hove Albion.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United vs Brighton TV channel, live stream, kick off time
arts.sum: Newcastle United return to Premier League action against Brighton and Hove Albion today looking for their first win in three league games - but with a chance to move into the top four. Eddie Howe's side have only lost once all season but will be keen to build on draws with Manchester City and Everton and continue their strong home form against a Brighton side who have also started well.
